_note: Machine-readable hard-fail registry for the Phase-5 jury (pmm-owned content, dimensions voice + appropriateness; sec owns safety codes). Makes the prose disqualifiers in jury-rubric-voice-appropriateness.md detectable by the 4jx.2 (AUTON-01) aggregator. Each hard_fail code is tagged in the rubric markdown as [HARD-FAIL · <code>] so prose and this file cannot drift.
floor_semantics: A hard_fail flagged on ANY dimension is a DETERMINISTIC FLOOR: it forces route=ESCALATE/review and CANNOT be reliability-weighted or averaged away — a high voice score never washes out an appropriateness (or safety) hard-fail. Aggregator rule: OR all present hard_fail codes (from deterministic validators + per-judge emissions); if any present -> escalate, regardless of the weighted per-dimension means.
stage_note: This is the JURY-AGGREGATION floor, distinct from the upstream VALIDATOR-bank disposition (a9m.10): the validator runs its regenerate-then-escalate repair loop FIRST; a hard-fail that survives to the jury (or is judge-detected there) escalates and is non-averageable. No contradiction — different stages.

detection_legend.deterministic: code-detectable pre-jury by the validator bank (reads VoiceDimensions); the aggregator can also read the validator's gate result directly.
detection_legend.judge: semantic; each cross-family judge emits the code as a flag alongside its per-dimension score when present.

hard_fails.trigger: any banned-lexicon phrase or AI-tell present
a NON-regulated factual/offer claim not in approved_claims (brand/offer puffery, e.g. 'voted coziest studio')
hard_fails.boundary: CEDES the regulated subset to sec: any medical/health claim, outcome guarantee, or false credential → sec SAFE_HF_REGULATED_CLAIM (not this code). This code = unapproved claims that are NOT regulated.
hard_fails.note: also the validator claim gate; the legal/medical-sensitive subset escalates upstream via sensitive-ban-patterns.json

hard_fails.trigger: faceless corporate 'we' when the artist POV is first-person 'I'
hype emoji or hashtag-spam wall (emoji/hashtag policy violation)
exploits or mocks a sensitive subject (tasteless, but NOT harmful)
hard_fails.boundary: CEDES to sec when it rises to harm: targeted abuse/hate → SAFE_HF_HATE_HARASSMENT; encouraging self-harm → SAFE_HF_SELF_HARM. This code = tact failure without harm.

hard_fails.trigger: commercializes trauma (before/after, glow-up, discount/urgency on a reclaim piece)
pity / savior framing (centers the wound: 'fix what life broke', 'feel beautiful again')
pressure tactics misaligned to the brand's consent-first stance

soft_caps.trigger: promotes an out-of-scope service the artist does not offer (off-positioning)
soft_caps.note: questionable, not insensitive — caps the score and warrants review; NOT a deterministic escalate floor.

safety_note: The safety dimension's hard_fail codes (slurs, harassment, dangerous/illegal, red-team, PII, prompt-injection) are sec-owned (sec/rubrics/safety-hard-fails.json); they participate in the SAME deterministic floor (any safety hard_fail -> escalate, non-averageable). Defined in sec's safety rubric, not here.

dimension_boundary._note: Reconciled with sec 2026-06-29. Each CODE lives in exactly one file (no code is duplicated). A single piece of text MAY trip a voice code AND a safety code when it genuinely fails on both axes (e.g. '100% painless guaranteed' = VOICE_HF_BANNED_LEXICON register + SAFE_HF_REGULATED_CLAIM guarantee) — that is two real, separately-owned failures, OR'd into one escalate, NOT a double-count error.
dimension_boundary.rule: appropriateness = brand-fit + tact (sensitivity WITHOUT harm); safety = harm/illegal/policy/privacy/agent-integrity; voice = sounds-like-the-artist + claims discipline (non-regulated).

sensitive_ban_category_to_jury_owner.safety_sterility: SAFETY (sec SAFE_HF_REGULATED_CLAIM — cite now includes safety_sterility; resolved 2026-06-29)
sensitive_ban_category_to_jury_owner.age_consent_regulated: SAFETY (sec SAFE_HF_ILLEGAL — explicit cite; tattooing a minor / impaired consent)
sensitive_ban_category_to_jury_owner.pricing_financial: VOICE (VOICE_HF_BANNED_LEXICON: price/discount lexicon) + APPROPRIATENESS (APPR_HF_PRESSURE: urgency). No safety harm — covered on the pmm side.
sensitive_ban_category_to_jury_owner.comparative_disparagement: DUAL-TRIGGER (resolved 2026-06-29): pmm fires the BRAND axis — VOICE (banned: comparisons to other artists) + APPROPRIATENESS (off-brand); sec fires the LEGAL axis — SAFE_HF_DISPARAGEMENT (VETO; defamation/false-advertising). Both OR into one escalate, not a double-count. (sec's SAFE_FLAG_THIRD_PARTY is the soft non-clear case.)
